name: Saint-Emilion underground monuments and monolithic church
summary: The most distinctive cultural stop in Saint-Emilion: guided underground monuments centered on the rock-cut monolithic church.
whyItMatters: It gives the Saint-Emilion wine day a medieval/UNESCO anchor and can be paired naturally with lunch, a cellar visit, or a village walk.
timeRequired: 45-75 minutes for the guided monuments; 2-3 hours with village wandering.
ticketOrReservationNeed: Guided-tour ticket required for underground monuments; book ahead in summer.
pros: Highly distinctive, not interchangeable with ordinary church visits
Pairs naturally with Saint-Emilion wine and food plans
UNESCO landscape context strengthens the stop
cons: Access is tour-controlled, so timing must be reserved
Village parking and crowds can be difficult in summer
Not ideal for anyone uncomfortable underground

logistics.opening_hours: Check Saint-Emilion tourism office tour schedule for the exact July 2026 date.
logistics.distance_from_base: About 40-60 minutes from Bordeaux by car or train in normal conditions, route dependent.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Separate from Stage 7 Bordeaux finish; best as a non-race day or post-stage wine-area day.
logistics.transit: Train to Saint-Emilion plus walk/taxi, or car with perimeter parking.
logistics.parking: Use official village parking guidance; do not assume close-in parking.
logistics.road_closure_notes: No specific TDF closure assumed in Saint-Emilion; verify local events and Bordeaux transfer constraints.

timing.best_visit_window: Dedicated Saint-Emilion half-day, preferably not the Bordeaux finish day.
timing.race_day_risk: Low in Saint-Emilion itself, medium if linked to Bordeaux race-day transfers.

nearbyFoodDrinkPairing: Pair with a Saint-Emilion wine tasting, lunch terrace, or macaron stop in the village.
